title: teucrium luteum subsp. latifolium
intro: Teucrium luteum subsp. latifolium, commonly known as the broad-leaved yellow germander or broad-leaved yellow tea, is a subspecies of the Teucrium luteum plant, a flowering plant in the mint family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Teucrium luteum subsp. latifolium include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antibacterial agent, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties, or used as a tincture for its concentrated medicinal effects.
